title: Project Metadata, Python Packaging, Entry Points, Plugins, and Clean Installs · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: An internal developer-platform release engineer. They need a threat-modeled generator, safe content and template boundaries, layered parser and browser tests, bounded fuzzing, actionable diagnostics, inspected wheel and sdist artifacts, a clean-install console command, and explicit plugin limits. Planned artifact: Deliver Secure Tested and Packaged Generator Release with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, ex…
objectives: Disable or sanitize raw HTML according to content trust and parser configuration and test dangerous tags, attributes, URLs, and DOM identifiers.
Separate scanner, parser, renderer, route, asset, incremental, CLI, filesystem, validation, browser, accessibility, deployment, and recovery evidence.
Fuzz malformed delimiters, nesting, Unicode, metadata, paths, templates, and assets under time, memory, depth, file, and diagnostic budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Project Markdown Html Policy
steps.instruction:
project case — A project publishing team handles case 3770 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to verify inspected package artifacts, clean command installs, resources, and plugin limits; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Disable or sanitize raw HTML according to content trust and parser configuration and test dangerous tags, attributes, URLs, and DOM identifiers.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Clean Install
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-clean-install-w20rwq" that uses case 5090 to verify inspected package artifacts, clean command installs, resources, and plugin limits. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Clean Install.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Clean Install into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Clean Install, keep Clean Install connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-clean-install-w20rwq
# Competency: ssg-clean-install.
# Case 5090: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_clean_install_w20rwq(artifacts: int = 2, inspected: int = 2, clean_installs: int = 3, command_smokes: int = 3, unsafe_plugins: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_w20rwq = "w20rwq-qwr02w-5090"
    assert evidence_variant_w20rwq
    evidence_axes_w20rwq = (
        "readerw20rwq",
        "source5090",
        "parseqwr02w",
        "routew20rwq5090",
        "artifact5090w20rwq",
        "recoveryqwr02w5090",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_w20rwq)) == 6
    if artifacts != 2 or inspected < 0:
        return False, inspected, "artifact-shape"
    if inspected != artifacts:
        return False, inspected, "artifact-inspection"
    if clean_installs < 2 or command_smokes != clean_installs:
        return False, command_smokes, "clean-install"
    if unsafe_plugins > 0:
        return False, unsafe_plugins, "plugin-boundary"
    assert inspected == artifacts
    return True, command_smokes, "installed-generator"
